serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Café de Paris Chicken Chop typically weighs approximately 400 g, comprising a grilled chicken piece (~180 g), mashed potato (~120 g), demi glace sauce (~60 g), cheese topping (~20 g), and a fried or poached egg (~60 g).

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, cooked, grilled; Mashed potato with butter and milk; composite dish estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy) with component-based estimation for composite dish
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Café de Paris Chicken Chop in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: grilled chicken breast (FDC ID 331960), mashed potato with butter and milk (FDC ID 170093), whole egg cooked (FDC ID 173424), cheddar cheese (FDC ID 328637), and demi glace sauce (estimated from standard brown sauce data). Weighted composite per 100 g of total dish (400 g serving): chicken ~45%, mashed potato ~30%, sauce ~15%, cheese ~5%, egg ~15% by weight. Values represent a plausible central estimate for this restaurant-style dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — mashed potato and mixed meat-potato meal analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Café de Paris Chicken Chop. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant glycemic contributor being mashed potato (GI ~54 per University of Sydney GI Database, boiled/mashed white potato) blended with a substantial protein and fat load from grilled chicken, cheese, egg, and demi glace sauce. Protein and fat co-ingestion is well-documented to attenuate postprandial glycemic response; the composite dish GI is estimated conservatively at 52 (low). Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (52 × 47.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 24.5, rounded to 24.7 accounting for sauce carbohydrate contribution. gi_category set to low per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~1520 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a standard serving and request reduced-salt demi glace sauce, as a full 400 g plate provides approximately 1520 mg sodium, representing over 65% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~24.7 per serving) driven by mashed potato
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the mashed potato component contributes a moderate glycemic load per serving; substituting mashed potato with a lower-GI side such as steamed vegetables or cauliflower mash and monitoring total carbohydrate intake (~47 g per serving) is advisable to manage postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese, butter in mash, and egg yolk (~3.2 g per 100 g; ~12.8 g per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the saturated fat contribution from cheese, buttered mashed potato, and egg (~12.8 g per serving) approaches the recommended daily limit of 14–20 g; choosing grilled chicken without cheese topping and requesting sauce on the side can meaningfully reduce saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Café de Paris Chicken Chop is a popular Singaporean Western-style dish served at local coffeeshops (kopitiams) and casual Western food stalls across Singapore. The dish features a grilled or pan-fried chicken piece — typically a bone-in thigh or a butterflied breast — topped with melted cheese and a fried or poached egg, served alongside creamy mashed potato and a rich demi glace (brown) sauce. The name 'Café de Paris' in the Singaporean context does not refer to the Swiss herb butter sauce of the same name; rather, it is a local branding convention used by hawker and kopitiam Western stalls to evoke a European bistro aesthetic. The chicken is typically marinated in a mild spice blend, grilled until golden, and finished with a slice of processed cheese melted on top. The demi glace sauce is a reduced brown gravy, often made from beef or chicken stock with aromatics, providing a savoury umami depth. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, high-protein meal providing approximately 178 kcal per 100 g and 14.2 g protein per 100 g, making it a satisfying protein source. The mashed potato contributes the primary carbohydrate load (~11.8 g per 100 g), while cheese and butter in the mash elevate saturated fat content. Sodium is elevated due to the demi glace sauce and cheese, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), moderated by the substantial protein and fat content of the dish. This dish is a staple of Singapore's affordable Western food culture and is commonly found at hawker centres, food courts, and kopitiams island-wide.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Café de Paris Chicken Chop was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for grilled chicken breast, mashed potato with butter and milk, whole egg, cheddar cheese, and demi glace sauce, weighted by approximate serving proportions. GI was estimated by analogy from mashed potato GI data in the University of Sydney GI Database, adjusted for protein and fat co-ingestion. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and restaurant-level variability in preparation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries; actual restaurant values may vary by ±20% depending on portion sizes, butter quantity in mash, and sauce volume. 2. The cuisine tag 'Indian' in the input JSON appears to be a field survey platform categorisation error; this dish is a Singaporean Western kopitiam dish and has been reclassified accordingly. 3. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on mashed potato analogue from University of Sydney GI Database, attenuated by protein and fat co-ingestion; gi_category set to low per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55). 4. Halal status is not confirmed; the dish may contain non-halal demi glace (beef stock base) and the restaurant (#Foodcoholic) halal certification status is unknown — is_halal set to false pending verification. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and are not directly reported for this specific dish; values are plausible central estimates. 6. No zero-value core macros are present; zero-macro justification rule does not apply to this record.
